title: The Holdovers
logline: A curmudgeonly classics teacher is forced to supervise students remaining at boarding school over Christmas break, forming an unlikely bond with a troubled teenager and the school's head cook.
antagonistOrConflict: Social isolation and the rigid institutional structures that keep people emotionally distant
tone: Bittersweet comedy-drama with moments of melancholy and warmth
themes: The cost of institutional loyalty vs personal integrity

actBreaks.description: The holdover students are picked up by their families via helicopter, leaving only Paul and Angus together for the remainder of break.

validation.ruling: Skip Smith's helicopter rescue fundamentally changes the story direction, leaving only Paul and Angus together and forcing them into the central relationship that defines the rest of the film.

actBreaks.description: Paul, Angus and Mary share a moment of genuine connection at the fancy restaurant, completing their emotional journey as a makeshift family before reality intrudes.

validation.ruling: Angus's devastating revelation about his father's mental illness and his own fears represents the emotional climax and point of no return, fundamentally shifting both characters' understanding and relationship.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a character-driven three-hander that uses the confined setting of a boarding school during Christmas break to force unlikely connections. The structure cleverly strips away external distractions - first the other holdover students leave, then the trio ventures into the outside world, creating intimate spaces for relationships to deepen. The screenplay builds through small moments rather than plot machinations, with each act defined by the evolving dynamic between Paul, Angus, and Mary. The pacing allows for genuine character development, particularly Paul's transformation from rigid disciplinarian to someone willing to sacrifice his career for a student's wellbeing.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The early scenes (1-36) establish the institutional world with quick cuts between locations, creating a sense of controlled chaos. The middle section slows significantly once the trio is established, using longer scenes to develop relationships. The Boston sequence (scenes 90-113) opens up visually and emotionally, giving the characters room to breathe and connect. The final act accelerates through the consequences, with shorter scenes building tension toward Paul's ultimate sacrifice.

incitingIncident.description: Dr. Woodrup asks Paul to supervise students over Christmas break due to Endicott's supposed family emergency, offering him a bonus for the assignment.

validation.ruling: Paul receives his assignment to supervise holdover students during Christmas break, establishing the main conflict and forcing him into the central situation that drives the entire story.

midpoint.description: At the hospital, Paul fears losing his job when Angus fabricates a story to the nurse about a hockey accident to protect Paul from trouble with the school.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. BARTON HOSPITAL - EMERGENCY ROOM - DAY

validation.ruling: Position sanity: original at 83.3%. Corrected to nearest turning-point in 35-72% window.

climax.description: Angus's mother explains his father's violent mental illness and the stepfather's demand that Angus be sent to military academy, while Dr. Woodrup agrees to withdraw Angus from Barton.

validation.ruling: Paul's insult to Woodrup is aftermath, not climax. Scene 130 is the true climactic confrontation where Angus's mother reveals his father's violent mental illness and Woodrup agrees to withdraw Angus from Barton - this is the peak tension scene that resolves the main story question of Angus's fate.

scenes.heading: INT. CHAPEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Six boys in coat and tie listen to a choirmaster who instructs them on singing, then leads them in a beautiful rendition of 'Oh Little Town of Bethlehem.'

scenes.heading: EXT. BARTON, MASS. - DAY
scenes.summary: Snow falls on the declining working-class town of Barton, Massachusetts with its taverns, churches, and shuttered textile mill.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARTON ACADEMY - DAY
scenes.summary: The Gothic spires and Georgian buildings of Barton Academy are shown, presenting an Ivy League atmosphere.

scenes.heading: INT. CHAPEL - DAY
scenes.summary: The choirmaster smiles and critiques the boys' performance positively, telling them 'Very good. Excellent.'

scenes.heading: EXT. QUAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Boys hurry across the frigid campus, some tossing a football as they hustle to class.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Danny the custodian shovels the walkway, struggling to keep pace with the storm before stopping to stare at the sky.

scenes.heading: EXT. FACULTY RESIDENCE - DAY
scenes.summary: A single light shines in a top-floor window of the faculty residence building.

scenes.heading: INT. FACULTY RESIDENCE - PAUL’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul Hunham, a rumpled teacher with a wandering eye, grades exams in his smoke-filled room, calling students 'philistines' before being summoned by Dr. Woodrup.

scenes.heading: INT. FACULTY RESIDENCE - HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Miss Crane delivers Christmas cookies to Paul and informs him that Dr. Woodrup wants to see him about Christmas break.

scenes.heading: INT. STUDENT DORMITORY - HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Boys in the dormitory hallway shout and dress hurriedly in formal wear as music blares from a record player.

scenes.heading: INT. STUDENT DORMITORY - DORM ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Angus Tully packs for St. Kitts and defends his black Speedo to mocking classmate Crandall, comparing it to James Bond's swimwear.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Head cook Mary Lamb leads kitchen workers preparing breakfast while noticing the falling snow outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. QUAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul crosses the snowy campus in his duffle coat as boys race past him toward the dining hall.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Professors Rosensweig and Endicott discuss how Endicott avoided holiday duty by claiming his mother has lupus, wondering who will get stuck with it instead.

scenes.heading: INT. HEADMASTER’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Dr. Woodrup asks Paul to supervise students over Christmas break due to Endicott's supposed family emergency, offering him a bonus for the assignment.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul is assigned the holiday duty that will drive the main plot

scenes.heading: INT. PAUL’S CLASSROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul returns a failed exam to each student individually, with most receiving F's except for one student who gets a D-plus, while students react with dismay.

scenes.heading: INT. CHAPEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Father Joe leads a festive chapel service with parents and students, asking them to pray for the less fortunate during the holidays, while Mary sits prominently in the front row near a memorial plaque for Curtis Ezra Lamb.

scenes.heading: EXT. ADMINISTRATION BUILDING – DAY
scenes.summary: Boys rush toward luxury cars with their luggage as Dr. Woodrup wishes everyone Merry Christmas, while Angus scans the cars with growing concern before being called for a phone call.

scenes.heading: INT. ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Angus receives a devastating phone call from his mother Judy, who cancels their St. Kitts trip at the last minute for a honeymoon with her new husband Stanley.
scenes.turningPointNote: Angus learns he must stay at school, setting up the central relationship dynamic

scenes.heading: INT. DORM COMMON ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul addresses the five holdover students about spending holidays together with courage and fellowship, as a red-eyed Angus arrives with his suitcase after learning his plans fell through.

scenes.heading: EXT. QUAD - DUSK
scenes.summary: The boys head to the Infirmary while complaining about being stuck with Paul, with Kountze smoking marijuana despite Angus's warnings about getting in trouble.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DUSK
scenes.summary: Paul visits Mary in the kitchen where she explains she's staying because she feels leaving would mean abandoning her deceased son Curtis, while Paul awkwardly tries to comfort her.

scenes.heading: INT. INFIRMARY - DUSK
scenes.summary: Angus accuses Kountze of stealing his photograph, leading to a heated confrontation where Angus suggests Kountze's parents don't want him home either.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING HALL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At dinner, Mary serves the boys chicken and potatoes while Paul compares their situation to Romans besieging Carthage, as Kountze sits apart without food.

scenes.heading: INT. BARTON ACADEMY - VARIOUS LOCATIONS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paul conducts his nightly security rounds with flashlight and keys, checking classrooms and other areas before noticing the cognac bottle in the headmaster's office.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK STAIRWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paul climbs stairs and hears television laughter echoing from above.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN STAFF COMMON ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul finds Mary watching The Newlywed Game while shelling walnuts and drinking bourbon, and she explains the show to him before pouring him a drink.

scenes.heading: INT. INFIRMARY - MORNING
scenes.summary: Paul wakes the sleeping boys by banging bedpans and shouting at them to get up.

scenes.heading: EXT. QUAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The boys run laps in the quad while Paul, smoking and coughing, urges them to go faster saying the body devours itself without exercise.

scenes.heading: INT. THE “SCHOOL ROOM” - DAY
scenes.summary: The boys study in a massive study hall while Paul reads at the proctor's table, with Angus secretly reading comics and a skin magazine inside his textbook.

scenes.heading: INT. MARY’S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Mary sits alone in her book-filled apartment doing a crossword puzzle while soft music plays, surrounded by family photos including many of Curtis.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVERBANK - DAY
scenes.summary: The boys walk along the riverbank as Smith leads with a football and smokes marijuana, while they discuss escape plans and Smith mentions his CEO father's helicopter.

scenes.heading: INT. INFIRMARY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Angus awakens to find Park crying from a nightmare and wetting his bed, then comforts him and offers to help hide the soiled sheets.

scenes.heading: INT. FOUNDERS’ ROOM LIBRARY - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul and the boys hear a growing roar and rush to the library window to see a helicopter landing on campus.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mary approaches her kitchen window after hearing the helicopter noise.

scenes.heading: INT. FOUNDERS’ ROOM LIBRARY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul and the boys watch as Harry 'Skip' Smith emerges from the helicopter, prompting Jason Smith to run out asking if anyone wants to ski.

scenes.heading: INT. ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: The boys watch through the office window as Paul makes phone calls, with Jason Smith giving a thumbs-up to signal his departure has been approved, though Paul's glance suggests Angus isn't so fortunate.

scenes.heading: INT. ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul tells Angus that his parents aren't answering at their hotel and are away on some excursion, while Angus desperately pleads for him to try calling again.

scenes.heading: INT. INFIRMARY - DAY
scenes.summary: The other boys pack and leave one by one, with Kountze mockingly returning Angus's photograph before departing, leaving Angus alone and devastated.
scenes.turningPointNote: All other students leave, isolating Angus completely with Paul

scenes.heading: EXT. QUAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul and Angus watch the helicopter fly away, both equally disappointed, as Paul suggests they make the best of their situation while Angus stares at the empty horizon with hurt and rage.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN STAFF COMMON ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paul, Mary, and Angus watch The Newlywed Game while drinking, with Paul revealing he came close to marriage once but came to his senses, describing himself as drawn to ascetic monastic life.

scenes.heading: INT. NURSE’S QUARTERS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paul, now in pajamas, takes a deep pull from a bottle of Jim Beam before collapsing heavily onto the thin mattress in the nurse's quarters.

scenes.heading: INT. NURSE’S QUART ERS - LATER
scenes.summary: Angus quietly enters Paul's room while he's passed out and takes his ring of keys and flashlight.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Angus raids the kitchen freezer and eats freezer-burned ice cream with a fork.

scenes.heading: INT. CHAPEL - SACRISTY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Angus pours sacramental wine into a chalice and drinks it in the chapel sacristy, finding it tastes good.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN STAFF COMMON ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Angus observes Mary sleeping on the couch in front of a television showing a test pattern.

scenes.heading: INT. CHAPEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Angus sits at the chapel piano, lights a cigarette, strikes a single key, and exhales smoke that hangs in the moonlight.

scenes.heading: INT. CHAPEL - DAWN
scenes.summary: At dawn, Angus contemplates Curtis Lamb's photo by the altar while Mary enters the chapel, and they acknowledge each other with nods.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul shares Christmas cookies with Angus and Mary at lunch, making exaggerated happy sounds while eating them, prompting Angus to ask to use the bathroom and stalk out.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Angus tries to book a hotel room by phone while Paul discovers him, leading to a heated confrontation where Angus calls them both losers and demands they stay out of each other's way.
scenes.turningPointNote: Open conflict erupts between Paul and Angus, establishing their antagonistic dynamic

scenes.heading: INT. TROPHY ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Angus races up stairs through the trophy room while Paul, out of breath, struggles to catch up.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW GYMNASIUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Despite Paul's warnings about the gym being off-limits and threatening to wash his hands of him, Angus defiantly steps onto the lacquered floor and runs toward a pommel horse.

scenes.heading: EXT. FACULTY PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul frantically scrapes ice from his car windshield while an injured Angus, wearing his coat with only one arm, urgently tells him to hurry.

scenes.heading: INT. NOVA - DAY
scenes.summary: While Paul drives to the hospital worried about getting fired, Angus argues that Paul is at fault for not supervising him properly, choking back tears.

scenes.heading: INT. BARTON HOSPITAL - EMERGENCY ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: At the hospital, Paul fears losing his job when Angus fabricates a story to the nurse about a hockey accident to protect Paul from trouble with the school.
scenes.turningPointNote: Angus chooses to protect Paul by lying about how he was injured, showing their relationship beginning to shift from antagonistic to protective.

scenes.heading: INT. BARTON HOSPITAL - TREATMENT ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: A doctor explains that Angus has dislocated his shoulder and demonstrates the painful procedure to pop it back into the socket using a bedsheet contraption.

scenes.heading: INT. BARTON HOSPITAL - PHARMACY - DAY
scenes.summary: At the pharmacy, Paul criticizes Angus for lying while picking up pain medication, but Angus argues he was protecting Paul from Woodrup finding out.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WINNING TICKET - EVENING
scenes.summary: Paul and Angus arrive outside a working-class bar called The Winning Ticket in the evening.

scenes.heading: INT. THE WINNING TICKET - EVENING
scenes.summary: Paul and Angus eat at the tavern where Miss Crane works as a waitress, and Paul buys beers for local guys who will likely be drafted to Vietnam while privileged Barton boys go to Ivy League schools.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARTON STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Outside the tavern, Paul tosses his keys to Angus and explains why he bought drinks for the local men, contrasting their fate with privileged Barton students who avoid Vietnam.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARTON STREET - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Paul struggles with his car door and has to enter through the passenger side in what appears to be a routine.

scenes.heading: INT. N OVA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: In the car, Angus bluntly tells Paul he smells like fish, and Paul explains he has trimethylaminuria, a condition that prevents his body from breaking down certain compounds.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING HALL - MORNING
scenes.summary: At breakfast, Mary questions why Paul and Angus missed dinner, and Danny the custodian mentions finding vomit in the gym, prompting suspicious looks.

scenes.heading: EXT. QUAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul takes a walk outside and finds a football in the snow, then throws it in an extremely awkward manner.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: In the kitchen, Paul helps Mary prepare Christmas dinner while she complains about serving difficult students, and Angus enters wanting brownies meant for the Christmas party.

scenes.heading: INT. INFIRMARY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Angus attempts to shave at a small sink despite having very little facial hair that needs shaving.

scenes.heading: INT. MARY'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mary stands in her closet choosing an outfit while Curtis's military dress uniform hangs among her clothes, and she touches a worn cardboard box above it.

scenes.heading: INT. NURSE’S QUARTERS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paul prepares for the party by putting on fresh clothes and using air freshener under his arms to mask his body odor condition.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW ENGLAND ROAD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Nova drives past Christmas-decorated shops and homes on a snowy New England road.

scenes.heading: EXT. MISS CRAN E’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Miss Crane, already drinking, welcomes Paul, Mary, and Angus to her Christmas party and makes suggestive jokes about Mary's brownies.

scenes.heading: INT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At the crowded Christmas party, Miss Crane introduces Paul to her family members while pointing out various relatives including her sister Kathy and nephew Marvin.

scenes.heading: INT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - BASEMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elise leads Angus to the basement where younger children are doing arts and crafts, and she discusses the purity of childhood art while Angus tries to impress her by mentioning Picasso's Guernica.

scenes.heading: INT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - DEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mary stands melancholically by the music while Danny approaches with a small gift, creating an awkward moment between them.

scenes.heading: INT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - BASEMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elise and Angus finger-paint together in the basement, and when she catches him trying to look down her shirt, she playfully acknowledges it and continues painting.

scenes.heading: INT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Miss Crane kisses Paul under mistletoe, then Paul awkwardly discusses mythology and reveals he left home at fifteen on a scholarship, never looking back except to return to Barton which feels like home.

scenes.heading: INT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mary breaks down sobbing in the kitchen, crying that Curtis is gone while Paul and Danny try unsuccessfully to comfort her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mary's emotional breakdown reveals the depth of her grief over losing Curtis, stripping away her brave facade and exposing raw pain that affects everyone present.

scenes.heading: EXT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paul and Angus help Mary to the car while Paul declares the party a disaster, but Angus argues he was having fun with Elise and wants to return, leading to Paul exploding that he doesn't want to be babysitting Angus.

scenes.heading: INT. NURSE’S QUARTERS - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Paul lies awake in bed staring at the ceiling, then sits up unable to sleep.

scenes.heading: INT. INFIRMARY - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul checks on Angus in the infirmary and finds him still asleep.

scenes.heading: EXT. FACULTY PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul trudges through more snow toward his car in the faculty parking lot.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The Nova drives past on a snowy road.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHRISTMAS TREE LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul visits a Christmas tree lot where townies offer him discounted remaining inventory from their pickup truck.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The Nova returns to school with a small Christmas tree tied to its roof.

scenes.heading: INT. INFIRMARY - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul discovers the infirmary empty and calls out frantically for Angus.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul finds Mary cooking with a hangover in the kitchen and asks about Angus's whereabouts, becoming frustrated when she hasn't seen him.

scenes.heading: EXT. BA RTON ACADEMY - QUAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul searches the empty quad, shouting Angus's name.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWAY - HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul continues searching through hallways and stairways, following the sound of music.

scenes.heading: INT. AUDITORIUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul finds Angus playing piano in the auditorium and asks where he's been before telling him he has something to show him.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul proudly presents a bare Christmas tree to Angus and Mary with three small gifts underneath, giving them each copies of Marcus Aurelius's Meditations and Mary a bottle of whiskey.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul's gesture of creating Christmas for them marks a shift from mere duty to genuine care, showing his growing emotional investment in their makeshift family.

scenes.heading: EXT. RESIDENTIAL STREET - ROXBURY, BOSTON - DAY
scenes.summary: The Nova pulls up to a triple-decker apartment building in Roxbury, Boston.

scenes.heading: INT. NOVA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul offers Mary a hotel room instead of the icy stairs, but she insists she needs a break from their bickering and wants to visit her pregnant sister, while Angus carries her bags including a special box.

scenes.heading: INT. PEGGY’S TOWNHOUSE - GUEST BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In the nursery, Mary opens the cardboard box revealing old baby clothes and carefully places the items in bureau drawers before Peggy appears and they embrace for a long time.

scenes.heading: INT. PEGGY’S TOWNHOUSE - GUEST BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Mary and Peggy sit on the bed talking and laughing while Lester smiles unseen from the doorway.

scenes.heading: EXT. BOSTON STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul and Angus walk through sun-dappled, snowy Boston streets.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANOTHER BOSTON STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul and Angus continue walking through bustling holiday crowds on Boston streets.

scenes.heading: EXT. BRATTLE BOOK SHOP - DAY
scenes.summary: At an outdoor bookstore, a prostitute approaches Paul asking for a cigarette and then a date, while Angus encourages Paul to go with her, but Paul politely declines.

scenes.heading: INT. BOSTON FINE ARTS MUSEUM - DAY
scenes.summary: At the art museum, Paul shows Angus ancient Greek pottery including erotic ceramics, explaining that human experience hasn't changed across generations.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - SKATING RINK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At a festive public skating rink, Paul watches from the sidelines as Angus skates laps, quietly marveling at the boy's vitality.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKATING RINK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Leaving the rink, Paul encounters Hugh Cavanaugh, a Harvard classmate who introduces his wife and mentions getting tenure, while Paul lies about his own success and current position.

scenes.heading: INT. LIQUOR STORE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In a liquor store, Angus confronts Paul about lying to Hugh, and Paul reveals he was accused of plagiarizing his Harvard roommate's thesis, though he claims the roommate actually stole from him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul's revelation about the plagiarism incident at Harvard explains his current circumstances and why he's stuck teaching at Barton, deepening Angus's understanding of his teacher's damaged past.

scenes.heading: EXT. BOSTON STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul reveals to Angus that he never graduated college and only Dr. Green knew, while taking a drink from a pint of Jim Beam.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul's deepest secret is revealed, fundamentally changing how Angus sees him and deepening their bond

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Angus drops a bottle of Librium pills that Paul discovers, with Angus claiming they're vitamins for low energy but Paul recognizing them as depression medication.

scenes.heading: INT. CANDLEPIN BOWLING ALLEY - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul and Angus go bowling, where Angus proves better at the game and coaches Paul's form while asking which of Paul's eyes he should look at when talking to him.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVIE THEATRE BALCONY
scenes.summary: Paul and Angus watch "Little Big Man" in a movie theater balcony, with Paul commenting on the film's accuracy until told to be quiet by another patron.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVIE THEATRE LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: Angus leaves the movie theater balcony but instead of going to the bathroom, he heads for the front doors.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVIE THEATRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul notices Angus's empty seat and reacts with alarm.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOVIE THEATRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul emerges from the theater and catches Angus getting into a taxi, confronting him and demanding he get out while Angus insists he was coming back.

scenes.heading: EXT. PSYCHI ATRIC HOSPITAL DRIVEWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: The taxi drives up a long access road to a psychiatric hospital.

scenes.heading: EXT. PSYCHIATRIC HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: The taxi arrives at a large stone psychiatric hospital building.

scenes.heading: INT. PSYCHIATRIC HOSPITAL CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: An orderly leads Angus and Paul through the psychiatric hospital corridor, with Angus entering a room alone while Paul waits on a bench.

scenes.heading: INT. PSYCHIATRIC HOSPITAL DAY ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Angus meets with his father Thomas in the psychiatric hospital day room, where his father appears heavily medicated and confused, not recognizing Angus.
scenes.turningPointNote: The devastating revelation of Angus's father's mental illness and the heartbreaking reality of their relationship

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Angus gazes sadly out the taxi window as they travel back toward Boston in the snow, with Paul watching him.

scenes.heading: INT. FANCY RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At a fancy restaurant, Angus tells Paul about his father's mental decline, his mother's divorce and remarriage, and his own fears about his future and lack of friends.
scenes.turningPointNote: Angus's emotional breakdown and confession reveals the depth of his trauma and isolation

scenes.heading: EXT. FANCY RESTAURANT PARKING LOT - NIG HT
scenes.summary: Paul, Angus, and Mary attempt to make cherries jubilee in a restaurant parking lot, but Paul adds too much alcohol and the takeout container catches fire.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW ENGLAND ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The Nova drives along a New England road heading home.

scenes.heading: INT. NOVA - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul, Angus, and Mary ride home in silence, each smiling faintly.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN STAFF COMMON ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paul, Angus, Mary, and Danny watch Guy Lombardo on TV for New Year's Eve, with Angus producing an M-80 firecracker that Paul won't let him use indoors.

scenes.heading: EXT. KITCHEN STAFF COMMON ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul lights the M-80 for Angus outside, and after the explosion, the three embrace through the kitchen windows as the scene fades out.
scenes.turningPointNote: The emotional peak of their found family bond before the return to school disrupts everything

scenes.heading: EXT. QUAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The cross-country team races across the quad as the new semester begins.

scenes.heading: INT. WEIGHT ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Barton crew team works out on rowing machines in the weight room.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW GYMNASIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: The basketball team runs drills in the new gymnasium.

scenes.heading: INT. STUDENT DORMITORY - DAY
scenes.summary: Boys get dressed and play in the student dormitory while Smith towels off his freshly-cut hair after showering.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Mary criticizes another cook for adding too much paprika to soup and instructs them to add more water.

scenes.heading: INT. PAUL’S CLASSROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul returns to teach his class where Kountze is sunburned from skiing, and Paul mocks him by comparing him to Icarus.

scenes.heading: EXT. ADMINISTRATION BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: A well-groomed couple in a Cadillac arrives at the administration building.

scenes.heading: INT. PAUL’S CLASSROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Miss Crane interrupts Paul grading exams to tell him that Dr. Woodrup urgently wants to see him.

scenes.heading: INT. HEADMASTER’S OUTER OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul arrives at the headmaster's office to find Angus waiting in the outer office, and they exchange a surprised look before Paul enters Woodrup's office.

scenes.heading: INT. HEADMASTER’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr. Woodrup introduces Paul to Angus's mother and stepfather, who confront Paul about taking Angus to Boston and visiting his father.

scenes.heading: INT. HEADMASTER’S OUTER OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Angus tells Mary that his mother and stepfather are there and he thinks he's going to get kicked out and sent to military school, and Mary takes his hand.

scenes.heading: INT. HEADMASTER’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Angus's mother explains his father's violent mental illness and the stepfather's demand that Angus be sent to military academy, while Dr. Woodrup agrees to withdraw Angus from Barton.

scenes.heading: INT. HEADMASTER'S OUTER OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Mary and Angus wait holding hands as they hear approaching footsteps.

scenes.heading: INT. HEADMASTER'S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr. Woodrup tells Paul he did this to himself, and Paul responds by calling Woodrup "penis cancer in human form."
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul's final confrontation with authority and his ultimate sacrifice to protect Angus

scenes.heading: EXT. HEADMASTER’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul emerges from the office and tells Angus which eye to look at when talking to him, smiling before walking away as Woodrup calls Angus inside.

scenes.heading: INT. FACULTY RESIDENCE - PAUL’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul packs his belongings in his faculty room and Mary gives him a leather-bound notebook for his monograph.

scenes.heading: EXT. REAR OF FACULTY RESIDENCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul packs his U-Haul trailer while students watch, with Kountze spreading rumors about why Paul was fired and Angus defending him.

scenes.heading: EXT. FACULTY PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Angus approaches Paul at his car to thank him for not letting him get kicked out, with Paul suggesting they could skip class to go eat, but Angus chooses to attend class instead.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final goodbye between Paul and Angus, with Angus making the mature choice to stay

scenes.heading: EXT. BARTON CAMPUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul's Nova towing the U-Haul drives past the Main Hall of Barton campus.

scenes.heading: INT. NOVA - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul drives away from Barton with the gothic buildings receding in the distance, his face showing both terror and hope at leaving his only home.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARTON DRIVEWAY / FRONT GATE - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul stops his car at the edge of the road at Barton's front gate.

scenes.heading: INT. NOVA - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul takes a swig of Woodrup's Louis XIII cognac, spits it out the window, and drives away from Barton as the camera holds on his car disappearing into the distance.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul's final act of defiance and departure into an uncertain future, completing his character arc
